title: pennisetum americanum subsp. typhoideum
intro: Pennisetum americanum subsp. typhoideum, also known as Elephant Grass or Napier Grass, is a perennial grass native to Africa, belonging to the Poaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditionally, its herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and essential oils, are used to treat various ailments, and it is also used as a food source for livestock and a natural remedy for soil erosion.
